name: zarr-python
tagline: Reference guide for using Zarr-Python 3 to create, chunk, compress, and access large N-D arrays locally and in S3/GCS cloud storage.
verdict: Solid, accurate Zarr-Python 3 reference: pinned installs, careful credential-handling guidance, and both bundled reference files resolve. Gaps: description never states a 'use when' trigger, no NOT-for/exclusions despite overlapping sibling skills (dask/xarray/h5py), and the 779-line SKILL.md runs over budget.

highlights.summary: Description lists capabilities (chunked arrays, S3/GCS, Dask/Xarray) but never states 'use when...' trigger phrasing.
No 'NOT for'/exclusions anywhere, despite real overlap with sibling xarray/dask/h5py skills referenced in the same doc.
779-line SKILL.md keeps Patterns/Issues/Performance sections inline; only API reference and v3 migration were split out.
